question: Based on the GDP map, which statement best describes the relationship between states?

options.A: All states have very diverse GDP levels.
options.B: Colorado and Idaho have similar GDPs.
options.C: The GDP gap between Colorado and Idaho might exceeds that between Indiana and Rhode Island.
options.D: The GDP differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Colorado (212.4$B) and Idaho (291.4$B) have a 79.0$B GDP difference, while Indiana (199.9$B) and Rhode Island (200.5$B) have only a 0.6$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Colorado and Idaho into the same color bin (200-400$B range), making their large difference appear similar, while Indiana and Rhode Island appear more different due to different color bins.
